description_long: This town hall complex features a collection of interconnected stone and brick buildings, with a prominent central structure showcasing a tall clock tower and ornate rooflines. The facades are richly detailed with arched windows, decorative cornices, and classical architectural elements, giving the ensemble a historic European character. The roofs are primarily steep and tiled, with some sections featuring dormer windows and gabled ends. The main building is flanked by several smaller annexes and outbuildings, some of which are simpler in form and darker in color, possibly representing service or administrative wings. The overall layout suggests a civic center with a grand entrance and multiple access points. The complex is visually unified by its use of stone, brick, and traditional architectural ornamentation.
description: A historic European-style town hall complex with a central clock tower, ornate facades, and multiple interconnected stone and brick buildings.
description_view_0: From this view, the full length of the town hall complex is visible, highlighting the central clock tower, ornate main building, and several smaller annexes and outbuildings.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the depth of the complex, with the clock tower and main entrance more prominent, and the layered arrangement of the buildings apparent.
description_view_2: The rear of the complex is shown, revealing the backs of the main and annex buildings, as well as the varied rooflines and additional outbuildings.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the opposite side, showcasing the large roof of the main building and the arrangement of the annexes, with the clock tower visible in the background.
attribute_architectural_style: Historic European, Neo-Gothic with Classical influences
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Arched windows, decorative cornices, pilasters, and detailed rooflines with ornamental gables and clock tower
attribute_entrance_design: Grand main entrance with double doors, arched framing, stone steps, and decorative columns; secondary entrances on annexes
